sub_mechanisms: M_1: write attention weight as f(cos θ) where θ = ∠(Q,K)
M_2: Chebyshev expand f to degree K
M_3: K-term truncation: store K coefficients per attention head
M_4: LoRA delta = perturbation of K Chebyshev coefficients
stripped_llm_application: Treat attention-weight as a function of pair-angle; expand in optimal polynomial basis to degree K; store K coefficients per head; LoRA update = perturb K coefficients; exponential convergence in K.
novelty_claim_stripped: Polynomial-Based Self-Attention (ICLR 2024) uses polynomial expansion of self-attention for graph-table representations; this candidate uses CHEBYSHEV-specific min-max polynomial basis with LoRA-coefficient perturbation.
